title: Informal Conjecture on exact asymptotics of maxcode_L for even L
statement_text: We believe that in fact $\mathrm{maxcode}_L(\varepsilon) = c_L \varepsilon^{-1} + O(1)$ for even $L$.
context_text: Theorem 2 establishes the two-sided bound $c_L \varepsilon^{-1} + O(1) \leq \mathrm{maxcode}_L(\varepsilon) = O(\varepsilon^{-1})$ for every even $L \geq 2$, where $c_L = 2^{-\lfloor L/2 \rfloor}\binom{L}{\lfloor L/2 \rfloor}$ comes from Theorem 1. The authors conjecture that the lower bound is tight up to an additive constant, i.e., that the exact leading coefficient is $c_L$.
notes: Stated as 'We believe that in fact …' in prose immediately after Theorem 2; no labelled theorem environment. PDF source — math notation may be garbled.
